{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about processing blood before transfusion to reduce the chances of febrile non-hemolytic transfusion reaction (FNHTR). Reviewing the documents, Document [2] mentions that leukoreduction, which is the removal of white blood cells from blood products, decreases the risk of febrile reactions, HLA sensitization, and CMV transmission. This aligns with the need to reduce the risk of FNHTR. Other documents discuss irradiation to prevent transfusion-associated graft-versus-host disease, washing to remove plasma proteins, and leucocyte reduction to decrease the risk of febrile reactions. Among the options provided, leucocyte reduction is the most directly relevant to reducing FNHTR.", "answer_choice": "C"}
